     The Numbers Business Report

Black Panther (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $700,059,566Details
International Box Office $634,097,516
Worldwide Box Office $1,334,157,082
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $34,166,965
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $70,464,907
Total $104,631,872
Further financial details...

Synopsis

King T’Challa returns home to the reclusive, technologically advanced African nation of Wakanda to serve as his country’s new leader. However, T’Challa soon finds that he is challenged for the throne from factions within his own country. When two foes conspire to destroy Wakanda, the hero known as Black Panther must team up with C.I.A. agent Everett K. Ross and members of the Dora Milaje, Wakanadan special forces, to prevent Wakanda from being dragged into a world war.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$202,003,951 (28.9% of total gross)
Legs:3.47 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:52.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$200,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,020 opening theaters/4,084 max. theaters, 9.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $919,068,317

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 16th, 2018 (Wide) by Walt Disney
February 16th, 2018 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
International Releases: February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (Taiwan)
February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (Indonesia)
February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (Philippines)
Video Release: May 8th, 2018 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for prolonged sequences of action violence, and a brief rude gesture.
(Rating bulletin 2511 (Cert #51390), 1/24/2018)
Running Time: 120 minutes
Franchise: Marvel Cinematic Universe, Black Panther
Award nomination: 2019 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Intertitle, Motion Capture Performance, Prologue, Scene in End Credits
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (city or town): Atlanta, GA, Busan, South Korea
Filming location (country): South Africa, South Korea, Uganda, Zambia
Filming location (locale): Atlanta City Hall, Atlanta, GA, Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, Uganda, EUE/Screen Gems Studios, Atlanta, GA, Gwangalli Beach, Busan, South Korea, Gwangandaegyo Bridge, Busan, South Korea, Haeundae District, Busan, South Korea, High Museum of Art, Atlanta, GA, Jagalchi Fish Market, Busan, South Korea, Marine City, Busan, South Korea, Rwenzori Mountains Uganda, Sweet Auburn, Atlanta, GA
Filming location (state or county): Fulton County, GA, Georgia, United States
Geographical setting: Fictional Country, Seoul, Africa, California, England, Korea, London, South Korea
Plot point: Arms Dealer, Dysfunctional Family, Magical Technology, Martial Arts, Meteorite, Museum, Non-Chronological, Presumed Dead, Royalty, Superhuman, Undercover
Social setting: Civil War, Navy, Rebellion, United Nations, World War III
Source material: Marvel Comics
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 1990s
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Marvel Studios
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

King T’Challa returns home to the reclusive, technologically advanced African nation of Wakanda to serve as his country’s new leader. However, T’Challa soon finds that he is challenged for the throne from factions within his own country. When two foes conspire to destroy Wakanda, the hero known as Black Panther must team up with C.I.A. agent Everett K. Ross and members of the Dora Milaje, Wakanadan special forces, to prevent Wakanda from being dragged into a world war.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$202,003,951 (28.9% of total gross)
Legs:3.47 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:52.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$200,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,020 opening theaters/4,084 max. theaters, 9.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $919,068,317

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 16th, 2018 (Wide) by Walt Disney
February 16th, 2018 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
International Releases: February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (Taiwan)
February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (Indonesia)
February 13th, 2018 (Wide) (Philippines)
Video Release: May 8th, 2018 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for prolonged sequences of action violence, and a brief rude gesture.
(Rating bulletin 2511 (Cert #51390), 1/24/2018)
Running Time: 120 minutes
Franchise: Marvel Cinematic Universe, Black Panther
Award nomination: 2019 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Intertitle, Motion Capture Performance, Prologue, Scene in End Credits
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (city or town): Atlanta, GA, Busan, South Korea

Filming location (locale): Atlanta City Hall, Atlanta, GA, Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, Uganda, EUE/Screen Gems Studios, Atlanta, GA, Gwangalli Beach, Busan, South Korea, Gwangandaegyo Bridge, Busan, South Korea, Haeundae District, Busan, South Korea, High Museum of Art, Atlanta, GA, Jagalchi Fish Market, Busan, South Korea, Marine City, Busan, South Korea, Rwenzori Mountains Uganda, Sweet Auburn, Atlanta, GA
Filming location (state or county): Fulton County, GA, Georgia, United States
Geographical setting: Fictional Country, Seoul, Africa, California, England, Korea, London, South Korea
Plot point: Arms Dealer, Dysfunctional Family, Magical Technology, Martial Arts, Meteorite, Museum, Non-Chronological, Presumed Dead, Royalty, Superhuman, Undercover
Social setting: Civil War, Navy, Rebellion, United Nations, World War III
Source material: Marvel Comics
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 1990s
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Marvel Studios
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
